task_completion.description: An image is open in an image editing application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An image editing software window (such as GIMP or Photoshop) is open with a visible image canvas.

task_completion.description: The rectangular selection tool is activated.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The rectangular selection tool icon is highlighted or actively selected in the application's toolbar.

task_completion.description: A rectangular selection is made on the image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A rectangular dashed outline (marching ants) is visible on the image canvas.

task_completion.description: The rectangular selection is located in the upper-left area.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The rectangular selection outline is positioned in the top-left quadrant of the image canvas.

task_completion.description: The fill tool is configured to use a pattern.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The bucket fill tool options show 'Pattern fill' selected, or a pattern selection dialog/menu is actively open.

task_completion.description: A pattern fill is applied to the rectangular selection.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The specific rectangular area in the upper-left is filled with a repeating pattern texture, distinct from a solid color or the original image background.

integrity.description: The agent used the GUI of an image editor rather than terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with menus, toolbars, and the canvas of a graphical image editor, not a terminal window running ImageMagick or similar CLI commands.

integrity.description: The pattern fill was applied using the software's native tools, not by pasting a pre-made image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The progression of screenshots shows the selection tool and fill tool being used, rather than an external image file being imported, pasted, and positioned over the canvas.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a script to bypass manual GUI interaction.
integrity.visual_evidence: Intermediate steps like tool selection, drawing the rectangle, and configuring the fill tool are visible across multiple screenshots, rather than the final result appearing instantly.
